title: Boogie/Dafny Verification Stack
summary: A three-layer program verification stack inspired by the Boogie/Dafny/IronFleet tool chain from Microsoft Research. The stack implements: (1) a BoogiePL intermediate verification language with weakest-precondition VC generation, (2) a Dafny-like high-level verification language that translates to BoogiePL, and (3) a distributed lock service verified using IronFleet's layered refinement methodology.
instructionPreview: Implement a three-layer program verification stack in Python using Z3:
instruction:
Implement a three-layer program verification stack in Python using Z3:

1. BoogiePL Verifier (boogie_verifier.py ~1500 lines): Implement a complete
   BoogiePL intermediate verification language with: full type system
   (Int/Bool/Bitvec/Map/UserDefined), typed AST hierarchy (Program, Procedure
   with contracts, Implementation with basic blocks, Cmd subtypes, Expr subtypes),
   tokenizer, recursive-descent parser, WP transformer (weakest precondition for
   assign/assert/assume/havoc/call/array-store), VC generator with SSA support
   and loop handling (back-edge detection via DFS, invariant injection as
   assert+havoc+assume, cut-point encoding), procedure verifier with modular
   contracts (free vs checked pre/post, frame conditions), SMT encoder (Z3
   integration + SMT-LIB2 format output), counterexample extraction, verification
   diagnostics, trigger selection heuristics for quantifiers, CFG analysis
   (dominators, natural loops, critical edges), and loop invariant inference.
   Verify test programs in test_programs/boogie/.

2. Dafny Translator (dafny_translator.py ~1700 lines): Implement a complete
   Dafny auto-active verifier with: rich type system (Int/Nat/Bool/Real/Array/
   Seq/Set/Map/Datatype/Codatatype/Class/SubsetType/NewType/TypeParam), full AST
   (Module/Class/Datatype with constructors, Method/Function/Lemma/Predicate with
   specifications, Statement hierarchy with match/for/calc, Expression hierarchy
   with quantifiers/match/let/old/fresh), tokenizer, recursive-descent parser,
   termination checker (decreases clauses, well-founded orders, structural
   decrease for datatypes, lexicographic tuples), automatic induction engine
   (detect inductive arguments, generate hypotheses), refinement checker
   (abstract module -> concrete module, signature compatibility), Boogie compiler
   (Dafny->Boogie translation: method->procedure, function->axiom, datatype->
   discriminator/destructor functions, loop desugaring to basic blocks, ghost
   erasure), type checker with inference, Z3-based verification with path-
   sensitive VC generation, and diagnostics. Verify test programs in
   test_programs/dafny/.

3. IronFleet Lock Service (ironfleet_lock.py ~1200 lines): Implement and verify
   a distributed lock service using IronFleet's layered refinement methodology:
   protocol state machine (State/Action/Init/Next), safety invariants (mutual
   exclusion as inductive invariant, epoch agreement, message invariant, epoch
   monotonicity), host implementation with IO actions (send/receive/timer),
   specification state machine (history-based), abstraction function (refinement
   mapping: holder->spec_holder, msg_dst->spec_holder), forward simulation proof,
   liveness proof (ranking function/variant that decreases on fair steps),
   system composition (multi-host with shared network, global invariant =
   conjunction of host + network + cross-host properties), extended network model
   (duplication prevention, message validity), environment model with fairness
   assumptions, temporal reasoning (bounded liveness, progress verification),
   invariant decomposition, fairness checking (weak fairness, deadlock-freedom),
   multi-step refinement (Grant+Accept=SpecNext), and epoch monotonicity proofs.
   All verified with Z3 bounded model checking (N=3 nodes).

Working directory: /workspace
Run tests: pytest in /tests/{boogie,dafny,ironfleet}/
Each module has a run.sh that executes all experiments and prints KEY: VALUE results.

moduleDag.description: A three-layer program verification stack inspired by the Boogie/Dafny/IronFleet tool chain from Microsoft Research. The stack implements: (1) a BoogiePL intermediate verification language with weakest-precondition VC generation, (2) a Dafny-like high-level verification language that translates to BoogiePL, and (3) a distributed lock service verified using IronFleet's layered refinement methodology.

nodes.label: Boogie: BoogiePL Verifier

nodes.label: Dafny: Program Verifier
nodes.description: Full Dafny auto-active verifier implementation including: rich type system (Int/Nat/Bool/Real/Array/Seq/Set/Map/Datatype/Class/SubsetType/NewType), complete AST (Module/Class/Datatype/Method/Function/Lemma/Predicate, Statement hierarchy, Expression hierarchy with quantifiers and match), tokenizer with keyword recognition, recursive-descent parser, termination checker (decreases clauses, structural decrease, lexicographic tuples), automatic induction engine (detect inductive args, hypothesis generation), refinement checker (abstractâ†’concrete module, signature compatibility), Boogie compiler (methodâ†’procedure, functionâ†’axiom, datatypeâ†’discriminator, loop desugaring to basic blocks, ghost erasure), type checker with inference, Z3-based verification with path-sensitive VC generation, and diagnostics.

nodes.label: IronFleet: Distributed System Verification
nodes.description: Complete IronFleet-style distributed lock service verification including: protocol state machine (State/Action/Init/Next), safety invariants (mutual exclusion, epoch agreement, message invariant, epoch monotonicity), host implementation with IO actions, specification state machine, abstraction function (refinement mapping), forward simulation proof, liveness proof with ranking function, system composition (multi-host with shared network), extended network model (duplication/reordering/dropping), environment model with fairness, temporal reasoning (bounded liveness, progress verification), invariant decomposition (local/network/cross-host), fairness checking (WF/deadlock-freedom), multi-step refinement, and epoch monotonicity proofs.

edges.label: Dafny compiles to Boogie as its verification backend
IronFleet uses Dafny to verify distributed system implementations
